headline: GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra advances mathematical reasoning capabilities significantly
body: OpenAI released GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra, which produced a proof of the Cycle Double Cover Conjecture and demonstrated 76% performance on DeepSWE benchmarks at 61% lower cost than competing models. For healthcare AI, this represents a leap in complex reasoning that could enhance diagnostic decision support and clinical research synthesis where mathematical rigor matters. The improved cost efficiency and reasoning capability suggest vendors and health systems should reassess their foundation model strategies, particularly for AI applications requiring nuanced analysis of clinical literature and evidence synthesis. The consolidation of Chat, Work, and Codex into a unified interface signals OpenAI's push toward integrated clinical workflows, though early feedback indicates usability refinement is needed before healthcare deployment.
